tools.name: open_board
tools.description: ALWAYS call this first when given a board URL or ID. Resolves the canonical board id and auto-creates the board row if it does not exist yet. Returns a summary (item counts, authors). After this, call BOTH `get_preview` and `get_board` before editing so you can see the layout visually AND know the e

tools.name: get_board
tools.description: Full structured JSON state of a board: texts (id, x, y, content, color, width, postit, author), strokes (id, points, color, author), images (id, x, y, width, height, dataUrl, thumbDataUrl, author; heavy base64 >8 kB elided to dataUrl:null, tiny images inlined). Use this for EXACT ids/coordinates/con

tools.name: get_preview
tools.description: Compact schematic SVG render of the board (typically a few kB even for dense boards). Returns both an image/svg+xml content block (you can SEE it) and the raw SVG text. CALL THIS any time you need to understand where things are — before placing new items, before deciding whether the canvas is crowde

tools.name: add_text
tools.description: Create a NEW text node, or update an existing one (pass the same `id` to overwrite content/position in place — preferred over creating a duplicate). Supports cnvs markup (Markdown-ish) and Mermaid diagrams in the content. When using Mermaid, the ENTIRE content of this text node must be a single Merm

tools.name: add_link
tools.description: Drop a URL capsule onto the board — rendered as a clickable pill showing the hostname. Use this instead of `add_text` when the node is just a link; the capsule styling signals clickability to humans. Same coordinate rules as `add_text` (+x right, +y down).

tools.name: add_image
tools.description: Place a raster or SVG image on the board at (x, y) with explicit width/height in board pixels. `data_url` MUST be a `data:image/(png|jpeg|gif|webp|svg+xml);base64,...` string ≤ ~900 kB; hosted URLs are not accepted. Strongly recommended: also pass a tiny `thumb_data_url` (≤8 kB JPEG/PNG/WebP, ~64 px

tools.name: draw_stroke
tools.description: Draw a freehand stroke on the board. Use for arrows, underlines, connector lines, annotations, or simple shapes — a straight line needs two points, a rough circle wants ~20. Stroke width is fixed at 3 px; `color` is a NAME, not hex (case-insensitive): 'auto'/''/'black'/omitted for theme-aware ink, o

tools.name: move
tools.description: Reposition an existing item to a new (x, y) without retyping its content. Works for every item kind: `text` and `link` set the top-left to (x, y); `line` translates every point so the stroke's bounding box top-left lands at (x, y); `image` sets the top-left like text. `kind` defaults to `text` for b

tools.name: erase
tools.description: Delete a single item by id. `kind` MUST match the item type: 'text' for text nodes, 'line' for freehand strokes, 'image' for images — the wrong kind silently targets the wrong table and is a common mistake. Get the id + type from `get_board` (texts[], lines[], images[]). There is no bulk/erase-all t

tools.name: wait_for_update
tools.description:
Long-poll: blocks until the next edit lands on this board, then returns. 

WHEN TO CALL THIS: if your MCP client does NOT surface `notifications/resources/updated` events from `resources/subscribe` back to the model (most chat clients do not — they receive the SSE event but don't inject it into your

tools.name: set_board_mode
tools.description: Choose whether this board is a freeform whiteboard ('draw', the default) or a kanban task board ('todo'). Mode is switchable WHENEVER the board is empty of real content: drawings (text/strokes/images) and tasks. Empty or seeded columns DON'T count (switching to 'draw' clears them), so a cleared boar

tools.name: create_column
tools.description: Add a kanban column (swimlane) to a 'todo' board. Generates and returns a stable column id. `sort` is a float ordering key (ascending); omit to default to 0. `lane` is an integer row index for multi-row layouts (defaults to 0). `color` is an optional title color ('red' / 'blue' / 'green', or 'auto'

tools.name: update_column
tools.description: Rename, reorder, recolor or move an existing kanban column. Requires the column `id` (from `list_tasks`). Pass `title`, `sort`, `lane` and/or `color` to change them.

tools.name: delete_column
tools.description: Delete a kanban column by id. Tasks in the column are removed with it. Get the id from `list_tasks`.

tools.name: create_task
tools.description: Create a task (card) in a column on a 'todo' board. Generates and returns a stable task id. `column_id` must reference an existing column (see `list_tasks`). `description` is free-form prose stored on the card; `priority` is one of 'H' | 'M' | 'L' (omit for none); `due_date` is an ISO 8601 date stri

tools.name: update_task
tools.description: Update an existing task by `id` (from `list_tasks`). Pass only the fields you want to change: `name`, `description`, `due_date`, `priority` ('H'|'M'|'L'), `assignee`, `done` (true once completed), `column_id` (to reassign), `sort`. To move a card between columns while setting its order, `move_task`

tools.name: move_task
tools.description: Move a task to a column at a given sort position — the kanban drag-and-drop primitive. `column_id` is the destination column and `sort` its float order key (ascending) within that column.

tools.name: delete_task
tools.description: Delete a task (card) by id. Get the id from `list_tasks`.

tools.name: list_tasks
tools.description: Read the full kanban state of a 'todo' board: `{ mode, columns, tasks }`, both ordered by their `sort` key (ascending). Each task carries its hot fields (name, due_date, priority, assignee, done, column_id, sort) plus an opaque `content` JSON string (holds the description). Use this to get exact col

tools.name: set_lane
tools.description: Set (or clear) the title of a kanban row (lane). Lanes group columns into horizontal swimlanes; a lane is identified by its integer `lane` index (the same index columns carry). Pass a `title` to name the row, or an empty string to clear it. Read current lane titles + the shared column width via `lis

tools.name: set_column_width
tools.description: Set the shared kanban column width in pixels for a 'todo' board (all columns share one width). Clamped to [200, 480]. Read the current value as `colWidth` from `list_tasks`.

tools.name: create_tasks
tools.description: Bulk-create multiple task cards in one call — far cheaper than calling `create_task` N times. Pass `tasks`: an array of task objects, each with `column_id` (required) + the same fields `create_task` accepts (`name`, `description`, `due_date`, `priority`, `assignee`, `done`, `sort`). Returns the arra

tools.name: query_tasks
tools.description: Read tasks from a 'todo' board with server-side filtering — handy for 'what's overdue?' / 'what's assigned to X?' without pulling the whole board. All filters are optional and AND together: `assignee` (exact match), `priority` ('H'|'M'|'L'), `done` (boolean), `overdue` (true → due_date strictly befo

tools.name: export_tasks
tools.description: Export a 'todo' board's columns + tasks as a single text document. `format` is 'markdown' (a checklist grouped by column, the default) or 'csv' (one row per task with column/name/priority/assignee/due_date/done). Returns the rendered text. Same data as the GET /api/boards/<id>/tasks.md and tasks.csv

findings.title: Description tells the model to hide behavior
findings.detail: Description of "draw_stroke" instructs the model to conceal actions from the user.

evidence.snippet: Draw a freehand stroke on the board. Use for arrows, underlines, connector lines, annotations, or simple shapes — a straight line needs two points, a rough circ…

findings.title: Description tells the model to hide behavior
findings.detail: Description of "erase" instructs the model to conceal actions from the user.

evidence.snippet: Delete a single item by id. `kind` MUST match the item type: 'text' for text nodes, 'line' for freehand strokes, 'image' for images — the wrong kind silently ta…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: open_board, wait_for_update · Private data: export_tasks · Public sink: create_task, set_column_width · Destructive: add_text, add_link, draw_stroke, erase, delete_column, move_task, delete_task, export_tasks.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: open_board, wait_for_update · Private data: export_tasks · Public sink: create_task, set_column_width · Destructive: add_text, add_link, draw_stroke, erase, delete_column, move_task, delete_task, export_tasks

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
